---
title: "S4-S6: Suspension And Warning Layer"
---

These pages are scaffolded for finite suspension/Euler parity and warning-aware higher-dimensional material.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

S4 through S6 are not presented as finished geometric worlds yet. The current learning goal is narrower: understand how finite suspension counts and Euler-parity facts can be reused across several higher-dimensional scaffolds.

:::

<div class="warning-box">
This section is a scaffold around finite suspension-count and Euler-parity facts. It does not claim a complete continuous S4-S6 geometry layer. The theorem card is the status source.
</div>

## Lesson Path

1. Read [Suspension Euler Parity](suspension_euler_parity.qmd).
2. Identify which facts are common finite suspension facts.
3. Treat quaternionic, projective, and octonionic-shadow language as roadmap material unless theorem cards say otherwise.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

What is the difference between proving a finite suspension-count fact and proving a complete theory of `S^4`, `S^5`, or `S^6`?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S456-T0001"></div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
